nodes.name: Content Request Webhook
nodes.notes:
ENTRY POINT: Receives content generation requests.

Expected JSON payload:
{
  "topic": "WordPress Security Best Practices",
  "keywords": ["wordpress security", "wp hardening"],
  "wordCount": 1500,
  "tone": "professional|casual|technical",
  "contentType": "blog|product|landing",
  "includeImages": true,
  "seoOptimize": true
}

Test: POST http://localhost:5678/webhook/content-generate

nodes.name: Validate Content Brief
nodes.notes:
VALIDATION: Prepares comprehensive content brief.

Sets defaults:
- Word count: 1000
- Tone: professional
- Type: blog post
- Images: enabled
- SEO: enabled

Adds structure:
- Introduction
- Multiple sections
- Conclusion
- Call-to-action

Calculates optimal section count based on word count

nodes.name: AI Featured Image Generator
nodes.notes:
IMAGE GENERATION: Creates featured image.

Only runs if includeImages = true

Model: DALL-E 3
- Highest quality
- Better prompt understanding
- Natural, realistic style

Settings:
- Size: 1792x1024 (wide format)
- Quality: HD
- Style: Natural (photorealistic)

Prompt includes:
- Article title
- Topic/theme
- Style requirements

Note: Costs ~$0.04-0.08 per image

nodes.name: Upload Image to WordPress
nodes.notes:
MEDIA UPLOAD: Uploads generated image to WordPress.

Uses WordPress Media REST API:
- POST /wp-json/wp/v2/media

Filename format:
- Sanitized article title + .png
- Example: wordpress-security-tips.png

Returns:
- Media ID (used for featured image)
- Image URL
- Thumbnail URLs

Handles:
- Large file uploads
- Proper MIME types
- WordPress media library integration

parameters.name: featured_media
parameters.value: ={{ $if($('Upload Image to WordPress').isExecuted, $('Upload Image to WordPress').item.json.id, null) }}

nodes.name: Create WordPress Post
nodes.notes:
POST CREATION: Creates draft post in WordPress.

Uses WordPress Posts REST API:
- POST /wp-json/wp/v2/posts

Sets:
- Title (SEO-optimized)
- Content (HTML formatted)
- Excerpt (summary)
- Status: DRAFT (for review)
- Featured image (from previous step)
- Yoast SEO meta description

Why draft?
- Allows human review
- Prevents auto-publishing mistakes
- Quality control checkpoint

Can be changed to 'publish' if auto-publish desired

nodes.name: Process Tags
nodes.notes:
TAG PROCESSING: Prepares tags for WordPress.

Converts AI-suggested tags to WordPress format:
- Name: Display name
- Slug: URL-friendly version

Example:
- Tag: "WordPress Security"
- Slug: "wordpress-security"

Next step will:
1. Check if tags exist
2. Create new tags if needed
3. Assign to post

nodes.name: Assign Tags to Post
nodes.notes:
TAG ASSIGNMENT: Adds tags to post.

Updates post with tags via:
- POST /wp-json/wp/v2/posts/{id}

WordPress will:
- Create new tags automatically
- Assign existing tags
- Update tag counts

No manual tag creation needed!

nodes.name: Generate Success Response
nodes.notes:
RESPONSE GENERATION: Creates detailed success message.

Includes:
- Content details (title, word count, reading time)
- WordPress links (view, edit)
- SEO information
- Performance metrics
- Next steps for user

Provides direct link to edit post in WordPress admin

nodes.name: Send Response
nodes.notes:
FINAL RESPONSE: Returns result to webhook caller.

Sends formatted JSON with all details.

Typical response time: 2-3 minutes
- Content generation: 30-60s
- Image generation: 30-45s
- WordPress API calls: 10-20s
- Processing: 10-20s

nodes.name: Parse & Validate Content
nodes.notes:
QUALITY CHECK: Validates AI-generated content.

Checks:
✓ Title exists and is reasonable length
✓ Meta description ≤ 155 chars
✓ Minimum word count met (100 words)
✓ Valid JSON structure

Adds metadata:
- Generation timestamp
- Actual vs target word count
- Request tracking ID

Fails gracefully if quality issues detected
